testcases.input: nums = [[1,2,3],[5,6,7],[9,10,11]] Output: 11 Explanation: The numbers 1, 3, 6, 9, and 11 are the only numbers present on at least one of the diagonals. Since 11 is the largest prime, we return 11.
testcases.expectedOutput: 11 Explanation: The numbers 1, 3, 6, 9, and 11 are the only numbers present on at least one of the diagonals. Since 11 is the largest prime, we return 11.

testcases.input: nums = [[1,2,3],[5,17,7],[9,11,10]] Output: 17 Explanation: The numbers 1, 3, 9, 10, and 17 are all present on at least one of the diagonals. 17 is the largest prime, so we return 17.
testcases.expectedOutput: 17 Explanation: The numbers 1, 3, 9, 10, and 17 are all present on at least one of the diagonals. 17 is the largest prime, so we return 17.
